claim.observation: Volatility3 windows.netscan output contains a TCPv4 row in LISTENING state bound to local address 0.0.0.0 port 3262 with PID 12528 and Owner subject_srv.ex, Created 2018-09-06 19:37:07.000000 UTC (with a matching TCPv6 :: 3262 LISTENING row for the same PID).
claim.interpretation: The process subject_srv.ex (PID 12528) is bound as a server socket on TCP port 3262 across all interfaces (0.0.0.0 and ::). Port 3262 is a non-standard high port not associated with any legitimate built-in Windows service, and the process name does not match any known Windows/OEM binary. This is consistent with an attacker-installed network listener / backdoor awaiting inbound connections.
